metadata.title: FaultRay — Zero-Risk Infrastructure Chaos Engineering
metadata.description: Prove your system's availability ceiling mathematically — without touching production. Pure simulation chaos engineering with 2,000+ scenarios.

hero.subtitle: Zero-Risk Infrastructure Chaos Engineering
hero.description: Prove your system's availability ceiling mathematically — without touching production.

problem.title: The Problem
items: Risk of real outages during testing

solution.title: The Solution
items: Zero risk — no real faults injected
Zero cost — runs on a laptop

challenges.heading: Challenges We Solve
challenges.subheading: FaultRay addresses the real operational gaps that keep engineering teams up at night.

items.solution: FaultRay automates topology scanning and resilience analysis — no tribal knowledge required.
FaultRay auto-scans your existing AWS / GCP / Azure infrastructure without requiring any IaC setup.
Prove DORA / SOC 2 compliance automatically with machine-generated evidence trails.

items.problem: Ad-hoc incident response with no runbooks
items.solution: FaultRay auto-generates runbooks and remediation scripts from simulation findings.
Automated METI / ISO 42001 compliance checks with audit-ready reports.

features.heading: Prove Resilience, Not Just Test It
features.subheading: Every feature maps to a concrete business outcome — not a checklist.

cards.title: 100+ Simulation Engines
cards.desc: Network, process, resource, dependency, latency, blast radius, SLA contract validation, and dozens more — powered by Monte Carlo, Markov chains, and queuing theory.

cards.title: 2,000+ Auto-Generated Scenarios
cards.desc: From single-node failures to cascading multi-region outages. Every scenario is generated from your topology YAML — over 2,000 unique scenarios for a typical 10-component topology.

cards.title: N-Layer (5-Layer) Availability Model
cards.desc: The only tool that decomposes your availability ceiling into five independent layers: Hardware, Software, Theoretical, Operational, and External SLA.

cards.title: AI-Powered Analysis
cards.desc: Claude-driven root cause analysis and actionable improvement recommendations ranked by impact and cost.

cards.title: DORA Compliance Reports
cards.desc: Generate audit-ready Digital Operational Resilience Act reports with evidence trails and risk assessments.

cards.title: Security Feed Integration
cards.desc: Automatically incorporate CVE data and NVD feeds to simulate vulnerability-triggered cascading failures.

cards.title: APM Monitoring (Real-Time)
cards.desc: Live performance metrics, trace correlation, and anomaly detection — integrated directly into your resilience dashboard with 35+ monitoring views.

cards.title: AI Governance (METI / ISO 42001)
cards.desc: Automated compliance checks against Japan's METI AI guidelines and ISO 42001 requirements. Prove responsible AI deployment with audit-ready evidence.

cards.title: Autonomous Remediation
cards.desc: Auto-generate runbooks, remediation scripts, and Terraform patches from simulation findings. Reduce mean time to repair from hours to minutes.

cards.title: 35+ Dashboard Pages
cards.desc: Full-featured web dashboard with topology editor, scenario explorer, N-layer drill-down, heatmap, DORA reports, and executive summaries.

agentResilience.heading: AI Agent Resilience Simulation
agentResilience.subheading: The only chaos engineering tool that models AI agents (LLM endpoints, tool services, orchestrators) as first-class failure nodes. Simulate how infrastructure outages cascade into hallucinations before they hit production.

cards.title: Cross-Layer Analysis
cards.desc: Trace how infrastructure failures (database down, cache miss) cascade into agent hallucinations. Expose silent degradation that looks healthy but produces wrong results.

cards.title: PREDICT · ADOPT · MANAGE
cards.desc: Three pillars for agent resilience: simulate chaos scenarios, assess deployment risk with blast-radius analysis, and generate monitoring rules automatically.

cards.title: 4 New Component Types
cards.desc: Model AI Agents, LLM Endpoints, Tool Services, and Agent Orchestrators as first-class nodes in your dependency graph alongside traditional infrastructure.

cards.title: 10 Agent Failure Modes
cards.desc: Hallucination, context overflow, LLM rate limiting, token exhaustion, tool failure, agent loops, prompt injection, confidence miscalibration, CoT collapse, and output amplification.

comparison.heading: How We Compare
comparison.subheading: FaultRay takes a fundamentally different approach

nlayer.heading: N-Layer (5-Layer) Availability Limit Model
nlayer.subheading: The only tool that decomposes your availability ceiling into five independent constraint layers

layers.label: Layer 1: Hardware Limit
layers.desc: Constrained by physical components: disk MTBF, network gear, power systems, failover promotion time

layers.label: Layer 2: Software Limit
layers.desc: Your actual ceiling: deploy pipelines, config errors, dependency failures, human error rate

layers.label: Layer 3: Theoretical Limit
layers.desc: Irreducible physical noise floor: network packet loss, GC pauses, kernel scheduling jitter

layers.label: Layer 4: Operational Limit
layers.desc: Incident response time, on-call coverage, runbook completeness, automation level

layers.label: Layer 5: External SLA
layers.desc: Hard ceiling imposed by third-party service availability (AWS, GCP, Stripe, etc.)

nlayer.whyP1: Most teams chase hardware nines while their software layer caps availability at 4 nines. FaultRay reveals exactly where your bottleneck lives so you invest in the right layer.
nlayer.whyP2: The N-Layer model is extensible: add Geographic, Economic, or custom domain-specific constraint layers to match your organization's unique availability boundaries.
nlayer.bindingDesc: When a simulation predicts availability exceeding any layer ceiling, FaultRay flags it and identifies the binding constraint layer as the target for infrastructure improvement.

quickstart.heading: Quick Start
quickstart.subheading: From zero to availability proof in 3 steps

roi.heading: ROI Calculator
roi.subheading: Estimate your annual savings with FaultRay
roi.disclaimer: Formula: Annual loss = Monthly revenue × (incident hours/720) × incidents × 12. FaultRay effect = 70% reduction.
